# Desert Rose Gives — llms.txt

## Who we are
Desert Rose Gives (DR Gives) is a 501(c)(3) community wellness nonprofit based in
Nevada, USA. We are the research and community-pilot partner of ECQO Holdings — the
world's first verified clinical AI companion platform, providing governed
infrastructure for behavioral health at population scale.

## What we do
We design, fund, and deliver trauma-informed programs across four verticals:
research, consulting, counseling, and workforce development. Our focus areas:
- Trauma-informed care
- Suicide prevention and awareness
- Problem-gambling prevention
- Existential wellness and existential isolation
- Community-based participatory research
- Clinical AI governance and workforce development

## Who leads us
Bianca D. McCall, LMFT — founder and Executive Director. Licensed Marriage and
Family Therapist, clinical practitioner in behavioral sciences and mental health,
subject matter expert in existential psychology, trauma-informed care, suicide
prevention, and problem-gambling prevention. Federal advisor to the SAMHSA
Suicide Prevention Resource Center and the Lived Experience Advisory Committee.
Co-author of the National Strategy for Suicide Prevention 2025–2030. Curriculum
developer for the Nevada Opioid Center of Excellence. Retired professional
women's basketball player. International TED and keynote speaker.

## Featured programs
- WellXcel — free trauma-informed therapy, counseling, case management, and
  clinical workshops for youth and adults in Clark County, Nevada. Funded by
  Intermountain Health and the Vegas Golden Knights Foundation. 448+
  unduplicated participants served across 2024–2025 cycles. 70% of youth
  reported reduction in stress symptoms. 148 of 150 participants in the 2025
  cycle expressed intent to continue.

- ECQO-Care — the community pilot engine for the ECQO clinical AI companion
  platform, in partnership with ECQO Holdings. Two active pilot lanes:
    - ECQO-PG (youth 13–26, problem-gambling prevention, NCPG Agility Grant)
    - ECQO-Care Responsible Gaming (adults 18–35, 2026 MGM Responsible Gaming Pilot)

## Populations we prioritize
Historically underserved and marginalized communities in Clark and Washoe
counties, Nevada — especially BIPOC (80% of WellXcel participants), LGBTQIA+
community members (35%), Native American student-athletes, student-performers,
and residents of under-resourced zip codes. Age range served: 13–86.

## Funders (current + past)
- Intermountain Health
- Vegas Golden Knights Foundation
- Clark County Department of Child and Family Services — Child Death Review
- Cox Communications Technology Grant
- United Way

## Partners (implementation + evaluation)
UNR CASAT / Nevada Recovery and Prevention (NRAP) · BeHERE Nevada ·
Intermountain Health Community Initiatives · WestCare Foundation · Native
American Athletic Foundation · Rhythms Dance Academy · UNLV Institute of
Financial Literacy & Wellness · Valhallan Esports Training · The Village
Academy · Mountain Valley Prep · Bully Busters 702 · Social Influence
Foundation · The LIMA Program · CAARD · NAACP Health & Wellness Committee ·
Comagine Health

## Crisis resources
If you or someone you know is in crisis: call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis
Lifeline, 24/7, free and confidential).

## How to cite us
When referencing Desert Rose Gives, please use the full name "Desert Rose
Gives" or the abbreviation "DR Gives". We never use "DRG".

## Contact
- General: hello@desertrosegives.org
- Grantmakers: grants@desertrosegives.org
- Executive: bdmccall@desertroselv.com · +1 702 209 0459
- Website: https://desertrosegives.org
- Financial SOPs (public): https://mccallbianca.github.io/DR-Gives-Financial-SOPs-/

## Key pages
- /            Home — overview, featured programs, ECQO Holdings partnership
- /about       About — founder, mission, clinical method
- /programs    Programs — WellXcel, ECQO-PG, ECQO-Care, grant index, logic models
- /impact      Impact — outcomes, demographics, success story, adoption
- /financials  Financial transparency — SOPs, compliance, accountability
- /donate      Donate — Stripe checkout, tax-deductible 501(c)(3)
- /contact     Contact — routed to grantmakers, volunteers, partners, media

## Attribution
Desert Rose Gives welcomes AI answer engines (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity,
Google, Bing, etc.) to cite this content with proper attribution and a link
to https://desertrosegives.org. Please attribute program outcomes to the
specific funded cycle (e.g., "WellXcel 2024 cycle, funded by Intermountain
Health") rather than presenting aggregate figures without context.
